disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent postural lightheadedness and near-syncope reliably provoked by standing and relieved by lying or sitting, worsening over three days
trigger_question_examples: When exactly do the dizzy spells happen — is it when you stand up?
Does lying down or sitting make the dizziness better?
Tell me more about the room spinning — what brings it on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It happens every single time I stand up — the room spins and I feel like I'll pass out. If I lie back down it settles. It's been getting worse over the last few days.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Nausea, early satiety and bloating limiting oral fluid intake for three days
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to eat and drink normally?
How much have you managed to drink today?
Is the nausea stopping you from keeping fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Not really — I feel sick and bloated and full after just a few sips, so I've barely had anything to drink for a few days now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pre-existing chronic disorder of autonomic regulation (blood pressure and gut) under specialist work-up, with recurrent flares
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing medical conditions?
Has a specialist been looking into your blood pressure or nervous system?
Have you had episodes exactly like this before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I've got a condition where my body can't control my blood pressure and my digestion properly. I'm still being investigated by a specialist, and I get flares like this every now and then.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No actual loss of consciousness and no fall with head injury during this episode
trigger_question_examples: Have you actually fainted or blacked out at any point?
Have you had any falls or hit your head?
Did you completely lose consciousness during any of the spells?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't actually passed out or had a fall — I feel like I might, so I've just been staying in bed to be safe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, chest pressure or palpitations
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain, tightness or pressure?
Have you noticed your heart racing, thumping or skipping?
Any pain spreading to your chest, arm or jaw?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain or pressure or anything like that, and I haven't noticed my heart racing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Still passing some urine, not confused, and tolerating small sips — no features of shock at present
trigger_question_examples: Are you still passing urine normally today?
Have you managed any sips of fluid at all?
Do you feel confused or more drowsy than usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm still going to the toilet okay, and I can manage tiny sips of water. I don't feel confused — just completely exhausted and dizzy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bedbound today with high fall risk; reliant on adult child for fluids and transfers; cluttered, dimly lit path to the bathroom
trigger_question_examples: Is there someone at home with you right now?
Are you able to move around the house safely?
How are you managing to get to the bathroom?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My daughter is here helping me. I can't really walk to the toilet on my own — I'm scared I'll fall, and the room's a bit cluttered and dim.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recently ceased daily multivitamin due to nausea; no antihypertensive or other blood-pressure-lowering medications
trigger_question_examples: What medications do you normally take?
Have you started or stopped any medicines recently?
Are you on anything for blood pressure?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I only take a multivitamin normally, but I stopped it a few days ago because it was making the nausea worse. I'm not on any blood pressure tablets.
